title: Bind quota pools and recover authoritative Grok billing
summary: Detect authoritative active models and keep model- or surface-specific quota pools from falsely exhausting an unrelated active route.
risks: Hermes auth.json active_provider can be stale and is not runtime routing truth
Session-level model switches can override configured defaults
Some CLIs do not persist an active model outside a running session
Unknown active models must remain unknown
A fully used optional model pool must not mark the whole provider exhausted
tests: Hermes model.provider and model.default parse independently from auth active_provider
Codex configured and current-thread model selection parses
Grok configured or active-session model selection parses
Cursor selectedModel binds to the matching Auto or named/API pool
Codex additional model pools affect availability only for the active model
Code-review and aggregate billing facts remain visible without blocking the active chat route
Request availability remains independent from utilization so explicit allowed, blocked, and unknown states are not converted into fake percentages
Validated current-period Grok CLI billing logs provide real weekly usage and reset facts with explicit freshness
